6549e618dd7828c4e244d59a.question: This nucleophilic (“NOO-klee-oh-FILL-ick”) compound attacks the carbon of an aldehyde in the first part of the Strecker synthesis. Due to solvated electrons, solutions of alkali metals in this compound produce a deep blue colour. Two of these molecules react with hydrogen peroxide to produce water and the rocket fuel hydrazine. An (*) osmium catalyst was superseded by an iron catalyst in a process to generate this compound that uses almost 2 percent of the world’s energy supply; that process, which involves breaking a triple bond between two nitrogen atoms, is the Haber-Bosch process. For 10 points, name this molecule with formula NH3.

An (*) osmium catalyst was superseded by an iron catalyst in a process to generate this compound that uses almost 2 percent of the world's energy supply; that process, which involves breaking a triple bond between two nitrogen atoms, is the Haber-Bosch process. 6549e61edd7828c4e244d7bd.leadin: This species was the first multicellular organism to have its whole genome sequenced. For 10 points each:
6549e61edd7828c4e244d7bd.leadin_sanitized: This species was the first multicellular organism to have its whole genome sequenced. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this organism that Fire and Mello used to discover gene silencing via RNA interference. The use of this species as a model organism was pioneered by Sydney Brenner.

Lab colonies of C. elegans are fed the OP50 strain of this bacteria. The Shiga toxin-producing O157:H7 strain of this rod-shaped bacteria is commonly found on ground beef and can cause food poisoning.

C. elegans is commonly used in the research of this phenomenon. Cells that bypass the Hayflick limit can avoid this phenomenon, which is accelerated in progeria and may be a result of telomere shortening.
